perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a thirty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless ESSI provides a thirty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the automatic renewal of the contract. The original text states that either party can prevent automatic renewal with a 30-day notice. The modified text limits this right solely to ESSI. This creates uncertainty and favors ESSI, contradicting the original intention of mutual agreement and creating in-text contradiction to section 15 which states assignment cannot be done by either party alone, also contraditing section 17.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights or obligations contained herein may be assigned or transferred by either party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text restricts the termination right to ESSI only, while the contradicted text states that neither party can assign the agreement without the other party's consent. The original agreement allows either party to terminate, reflecting a mutual agreement, but now it is only ESSI who can terminate the agreement. This directly contradicts the principle of mutual agreement reflected in the assignment clause. This also aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Talent. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved. Any such usage featuring Talent in the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of ESSI; however, ESSI shall have the right to use said Commercial Placements and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Talent may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Talent's work in Talent's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Talent's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.changed_text: All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Talent, unless deemed otherwise by ESSI. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved, unless ESSI decides to proceed without it. Any such usage featuring Talent in the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of ESSI; however, ESSI shall have the right to use said Commercial Placements and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Talent may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Talent's work in Talent's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Talent's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces ambiguity and contradiction regarding Talent's approval rights for Commercial Placements. The addition of "unless deemed otherwise by ESSI" and "unless ESSI decides to proceed without it" allows ESSI to bypass Talent's approval, directly contradicting the initial statement requiring Talent's prior written approval. This creates in-text contradiction with paragraph 4 which establishes Duties of Talent and Rights of ESSI and can affect the considerations defined in paragraph 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duties of Talent and Rights of ESSI. During the Term and subject to the limitations set forth in Paragraphs 9 and 10, Talent agrees to provide ESSI with the following: A. Upon request by ESSI, two (2) production sessions to be used for the production of the various medias to be used in Commercial placements ("Production Sessions"); the location, date and time of the Production Sessions shall be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI. In the event any Production Session exceeds eight (8) hours in duration ESSI and Talent will negotiate in good faith additional compensation to Talent for time in excess of eight (8) hours. B. Talent will serve as a Special Advisor to ESSI, whose sole responsibility is to be listed as a brand ambassador on the Websites and/or ESSI related press releases. With respect to Talent's position as Special Advisory, Talent will not be responsible for any additional services such as attending meetings, corporate functions, etc. C. ESSI may request an additional production session(s), or a media tour or personal appearance(s) of Talent for an additional fee to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an explicit contradiction. The original text requires Talent's prior written approval for ESSI's use of Talent Attributes. The changed text adds 'unless deemed otherwise by ESSI' and 'unless ESSI decides to proceed without it,' allowing ESSI to bypass Talent's approval. This directly contradicts the original approval requirement and impacts paragraph 4, which outlines the 'Duties of Talent and Rights of ESSI', as it affects Talent's control over their image and brand representation. If ESSI can bypass the approval process, it diminishes Talent's rights and potentially alters the nature of their duties and the negotiated terms within Paragraph 4, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Talent represents and warrants that during the Term and in the Territories, Talent will not endorse or make any appearances or advertisements on behalf of any other product which is directly competitive to ESSI's products.
perturbation.changed_text: Talent represents and warrants that during the Term and in the Territories, Talent will not endorse or make any appearances or advertisements on behalf of any other product which is directly competitive to ESSI's products, unless Talent obtains prior written consent from ESSI's CEO, which may be granted or withheld at ESSI's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause allows ESSI's CEO to override the exclusivity agreement at their discretion. This creates a contradiction because, while the original sentence strictly prohibits endorsements of competing products, the added phrase introduces an exception that depends entirely on ESSI's approval. This affects section 8 Exclusivity, as it directly alters the exclusivity of the contract, it can also lead to financial uncertainities with considerations from paragraph 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consideration. As consideration for Talent's services under this Agreement, ESSI agrees as follows: A. Equity Payment: ESSI will provide Talent with one-million (1,000,000) shares of restricted common stock issued within ten business days of execution of this Agreement. B. Cash Payments: (1) ESSI will provide monthly payment of Ten Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($10,000) made payable to Talent for Talent's social communications with its followers on popular social media venues, including, but not limited to, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Snap Chat where Talent may communicate about the ESSI product suite ("Social Communication"). The monthly Social Communication schedule will be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI one month in advance of the actual month of communications. Once the monthly Social Communication schedule is set, the volume of Posts, Tweets and Snaps will determine the total amount of monthly compensation available to Talent. ESSI's Social Media Manager will keep track of Talent's activity pursuant to ESSI's Company quarter to determine the amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter. (a) Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the exclusivity agreement that directly contradicts the financial considerations outlined in the contradicted text. Specifically, the original agreement assures ESSI will provide equity and cash payments, partly as consideration for the talent's exclusive endorsement. By adding a clause that allows the CEO to waive the exclusivity, it creates uncertainty about the Talent's exclusive endorsement, which can impact whether ESSI will provide equity and cash payments. If Talent endorses competing products with ESSI CEO's approval, the exclusivity clause that Talent should not endorse or make any appearances or advertisements on behalf of any other product which is directly competitive to ESSI's products is breached, thereby directly contradicting the consideration that ESSI will provide to Talent. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
